sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Stephanie Morales, is a 36-year-old female presenting with complaints of ankle pain after a fall on the outside of her ankle 2 days ago.
She reports that she was walking to her car and slipped on snow and ice, causing her ankle to twist underneath her.
She denies any bleeding and describes the pain as severe, with swelling and difficulty walking.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of diabetes and high blood pressure.
She is currently taking metformin and norvasc.
Review of systems: The patient denies any pain or symptoms in her calf, lower leg, or knee.
She reports that the pain is localized to the outside of her ankle.
Current medications:
Metformin
Norvasc
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure is 120/70 mmHg.
Physical exam: The patient has tenderness over the lateral malleolus and the fifth metatarsal on her left ankle.
There is swelling and redness present, with pain with valgus stressing of the ankle.
The patient has normal sensation and pulses in her ankle.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has an ankle sprain, but further evaluation with an x-ray is necessary to rule out a fracture.
Problem list: Ankle sprain; possible fracture; diabetes; high blood pressure.
Plan:
I ordered an x-ray of the patient's ankle to rule out a fracture.
I put her in an air splint and prescribed crutches to avoid weightbearing.
I prescribed naprosyn 500mg twice a day for pain control.
I also instructed the patient to keep her ankle elevated and apply ice to the affected area.
If the x-ray shows a fracture, I will refer her to orthopedics.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the x-ray results are available.
I will also schedule a follow-up appointment to check on her ankle and provide further instructions.
